metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5503
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 2D-E): E shows specificity of antibodies against Prune2 isoform 5, here referred to as Olfaxin. Immunoblot analysis of HEK293 cells transfected with FLAG-tagged mouse Olfaxin or Caytaxin with shRNA targeting exon 26 of mice Olfaxin (left panel). The 52-kD signal was decreased by siRNA targeting exon 11 of human Olfaxin.

Noteworthy is that in D, it is shown that anti-Olfaxin antibodies also detected higher molecular weight bands, which may be Prune2 isoforms or other cross-reactive proteins. Whether these bands remain after shRNA knockdown with Olfaxin is not shown.

Figure 5A) Confocal micrographs of double immunofluorescence staining of Olfaxin andVGLUT1 in primary neurons of mouse OB shows colocalization of Olfaxin and VLGUT1.

Figure 5D) Subcellular fractionation of whole brain lysate shows that Olfaxin is most prominently present in LS2, P2 and LP2.

Figure 5E) Immunogold electron microscopy of Olfaxin (arrowheads) in adult mouse piriform cortex shows Olfaxin localization to the presynapse.

Taken together, the paper shows the localiztion of Prune2 isoform 5 Olfaxin to the presynapse. Cross-reactivity of the antibody used in the assays could not be excluded.. Experimental description: Antibodies against Prune2 isoform 5, here referred to as Olfaxin was generated. In Figure 2C, epitopes used for generation of antibodies, anti-OlfN antibody against human amino acid sequence, and anti-Olf-C antibody against mouse amino acid sequence are underlined.

OB from E14.5 C57BL/6J mice was trypsinized and dissociated by gentle trituration.
